verdict: DO IT — with one correction to the review's file list. Five of the six candidates are genuinely dead by dynamic reachability and should be deleted outright: src/utils/parallel-processor.ts, src/utils/bundle-optimizer.ts, src/utils/memory-profiler.ts, src/runtime/bridge-core.ts, and src/runtime/optimized-node.ts. The sixth, src/runtime/protocol.ts, is NOT fully dead: its TYWRAP_PROTOCOL string const is dead (only bridge-core uses it), but its TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION numeric const has LIVE consumers (src/runtime/bridge-protocol.ts at lines 92/94/163 and test/runtime_node.test.ts:221). So protocol.ts must be drained-and-relocated, not blindly deleted: move TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION into transport.ts next to PROTOCOL_ID, drop TYWRAP_PROTOCOL entirely (it duplicates PROTOCOL_ID='tywrap/1'), then delete protocol.ts. Net ~2,400 lines of src plus ~1,300 lines of tests removed, the only utils->core layering inversion eliminated, and the protocol constants consolidated to one home. This is a clean, high-leverage subtraction with zero public-API loss because index.ts never exported any of these symbols.
verifiedSignals: DEAD: src/utils/parallel-processor.ts — only src caller is src/tywrap.ts:21 (import) + :48 (globalParallelProcessor.setDebug). grep for globalParallelProcessor.<anything-else> across src/ and test/ returns ONLY the setDebug call; init()/analyzeParallel()/generate methods are never invoked in production. Not exported from src/index.ts (verified full file, lines 1-157).

DEAD: src/runtime/bridge-core.ts — BridgeCore and normalizeEnv have ZERO src consumers (grep 'BridgeCore|normalizeEnv' across src/ excluding bridge-core.ts itself = empty). Only consumer is test/bridge-core.test.ts:3. Confirmed src/runtime/bridge-protocol.ts has zero references to bridge-core (grep -c = 0). The live protocol path is BridgeProtocol+Transport, not BridgeCore.

DEAD: src/runtime/optimized-node.ts — pure re-export shim (NodeBridge as OptimizedNodeBridge). No production import (only its own docstring references the path). All test consumers (test/optimized-node.test.ts:5, test/runtime_bridge_fixtures.test.ts:8, test/performance-integration.test.skip.ts:17) already import NodeBridge from src/runtime/node.js, NOT from the shim. Not in package.json exports map (verified lines 8-30: only ., ./node, ./pyodide, ./http, ./runtime, ./dev).

DEAD: src/utils/bundle-optimizer.ts — zero src consumers (grep 'bundle-optimizer|BundleOptimizer' across src/ excluding self = empty). Only test/performance-integration.test.ts:2 (live, 55 lines, tests private AST methods via 'as any') and test/performance-integration.test.skip.ts:20 (skipped).

DEAD: src/utils/memory-profiler.ts — zero src consumers (grep 'memory-profiler|MemoryProfiler' across src/ excluding self = empty). Only test/performance-integration.test.skip.ts:19 (skipped, never runs). The bare require('perf_hooks') at :491 is a TRUE-POSITIVE ESM crash but is reached only via setupGCTracking() (called from constructor at :89), wrapped in try/catch at :488, and the file is never imported in production — so it never executes, confirming dead.

PARTIALLY DEAD: src/runtime/protocol.ts — TYWRAP_PROTOCOL (string 'tywrap/1', :1) is consumed ONLY by dead bridge-core.ts:75,394, so it dies with bridge-core. TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION (number 1, :2) is LIVE: consumed by src/runtime/bridge-protocol.ts:92,94,163 and test/runtime_node.test.ts:221. Verified TYWRAP_PROTOCOL is an exact duplicate of transport.ts:20 PROTOCOL_ID='tywrap/1'.

LAYERING INVERSION confirmed (REVIEW B2): src/utils/parallel-processor.ts:14-15 imports CodeGenerator from ../core/generator.js and PyAnalyzer from ../core/analyzer.js — the only utils->core edge; vanishes on deletion.

index.ts barrel does NOT mask these: verified full src/index.ts (1-157) exports none of parallel-processor/bundle-optimizer/memory-profiler/bridge-core/protocol/optimized-node symbols. ts-fx deadcode blindness does not apply here because these were never in the barrel — they are dead despite that, reachable only via setDebug or tests.
falsePositives: REVIEW.md:68/B1 claims parallel-processor 'eagerly instantiates a global + 100ms setInterval over an empty queue'. PARTIALLY WRONG: the global IS instantiated at parallel-processor.ts:955, but the setInterval(...,100) at :661 lives in processQueue(), which is only called from init() (:190). init() is never called in production (only setDebug is). So at import time NO timer starts — the constructor (:146-167) has no timer side effect. Still dead, but the 'idle 100ms pump runs in prod' framing is inaccurate; also the interval is unref'd (:672) so it would not keep the loop alive even if started.

SIGNAL-DIGEST line 14 frames memory-profiler.ts:491 bare require as something that 'would throw ReferenceError under ESM'. Technically true, but understated as evidence: the require is inside a try/catch (:488), so even if the file ran under ESM it would NOT crash — GC tracking would silently no-op. The stronger proof of death is that the file has zero production importers at all.

REVIEW.md Quick Win 1 / B1 lists protocol.ts as a flat delete ('Remove ... protocol.ts'). This is WRONG as written — it would break src/runtime/bridge-protocol.ts and test/runtime_node.test.ts, which consume TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION. The constant must be relocated to transport.ts first. The review's separate instruction to 'collapse TYWRAP_PROTOCOL into PROTOCOL_ID' is correct but only covers the string const, not the version const.
proposal: Target state: src/utils/ loses parallel-processor.ts, bundle-optimizer.ts, memory-profiler.ts (cache.ts and logger.ts STAY — both have many live consumers: tywrap.ts, core/analyzer.ts, core/generator.ts, runtime/node.ts, cli.ts, core/discovery.ts). src/runtime/ loses bridge-core.ts and optimized-node.ts. Protocol constants are consolidated into src/runtime/transport.ts: keep PROTOCOL_ID='tywrap/1' (existing :20) and ADD `export const PROTOCOL_VERSION = 1;` next to it; src/runtime/bridge-protocol.ts imports PROTOCOL_VERSION from './transport.js' instead of TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION from './protocol.js'; then src/runtime/protocol.ts is deleted. src/tywrap.ts loses the import at :21 and the call at :48 (the `tywrap()` factory's globalParallelProcessor.setDebug becomes a no-op removal; globalCache.setDebug at :47 stays). Result: zero utils->core edges, one canonical protocol-constant home, ~2,400 src lines + ~1,300 test lines removed, no change to the public API surface (index.ts and package.json exports map unchanged except they never referenced these).
breakingChanges: Deep-import path src/runtime/optimized-node.ts (compile-time OptimizedNodeBridge/ProcessPoolOptions aliases) is removed. It is NOT in the package.json exports map, so it was never a supported public import; the public alias note in README.md:102 and docs/guide/runtimes/node.md:23 must be updated/removed. Migration: import { NodeBridge, type NodeBridgeOptions } from 'tywrap/node'.

Internal symbols ParallelProcessor/globalParallelProcessor, BundleOptimizer, MemoryProfiler/globalMemoryProfiler, BridgeCore/normalizeEnv, and the protocol.ts module are removed. None were exported from src/index.ts or any exports-map entry, so no documented public consumer breaks; only direct deep-importers (none exist in this repo) would.

src/runtime/protocol.ts is deleted and its TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION export moves to transport.ts as PROTOCOL_VERSION. Any external code importing TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION from 'tywrap/.../protocol.js' breaks (not part of exports map; test/runtime_node.test.ts must update its import). TYWRAP_PROTOCOL string const is removed entirely (use PROTOCOL_ID).

The tywrap() factory no longer toggles parallel-processor debug. Observable only if someone relied on debug logging from the never-initialized global processor (no one can, since init() was never called).
verificationPlan: Behavior-preserving for production (these files never execute on the generate/runtime path). Prove via: (1) before deletion, re-confirm reachability with `grep -rn` for each symbol across src/ excluding the file itself — must be empty for ParallelProcessor(beyond setDebug)/BundleOptimizer/MemoryProfiler/BridgeCore/normalizeEnv (already done). (2) After edits run `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit) and `npm run build` — must compile with no missing-import errors (the only cross-file edit is bridge-protocol.ts switching to transport.ts for the version const). (3) Run `npm test` — expect the deleted test files (bridge-core.test.ts, optimized-node.test.ts, performance-integration.test.ts) to be gone and the rest green; runtime_node.test.ts:221 must still assert against the relocated PROTOCOL_VERSION (=1, unchanged value). (4) Run `npm run check:all`. (5) Re-run ts-fx complexity scan — the four DEAD-CODE-tagged hotspots (bundle-optimizer.ts:238 visit, parallel-processor.ts:535 handleWorkerMessage) should disappear from the inventory, and the layering check should show zero utils->core edges. (6) grep docs/ for OptimizedNodeBridge to ensure the alias note is updated (G7 migration guide candidate).
steps: In src/runtime/transport.ts, add `export const PROTOCOL_VERSION = 1;` immediately after PROTOCOL_ID (line 20).

In src/runtime/bridge-protocol.ts, change the import at line 24 from `import { TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION } from './protocol.js';` to `import { PROTOCOL_VERSION } from './transport.js';` (merge into the existing transport.js import on line 25), and replace the three TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION references at :92, :94, :163 with PROTOCOL_VERSION.
In test/runtime_node.test.ts, change the import at line 14 to pull PROTOCOL_VERSION from '../src/runtime/transport.js' and update the assertion at :221.
In src/tywrap.ts, delete the import at line 21 (`import { globalParallelProcessor } ...`) and the call at line 48 (`globalParallelProcessor.setDebug(...)`); leave globalCache.setDebug at :47 intact.
Delete src/utils/parallel-processor.ts, src/utils/bundle-optimizer.ts, src/utils/memory-profiler.ts, src/runtime/bridge-core.ts, src/runtime/optimized-node.ts.

Delete test/bridge-core.test.ts, test/optimized-node.test.ts, test/performance-integration.test.ts, test/performance-integration.test.skip.ts. (optimized-node.test.ts and runtime_bridge_fixtures.test.ts both import NodeBridge directly; runtime_bridge_fixtures.test.ts is NOT deleted — keep it, it tests NodeBridge under the OptimizedNodeBridge alias name and remains valid.)
Update README.md:102 and docs/guide/runtimes/node.md:23 (and docs/public/llms-full.txt:1116) to drop the OptimizedNodeBridge deprecated-alias note, pointing to NodeBridge pooling options instead.
Run npm run typecheck && npm run build && npm test && npm run check:all; confirm green.
Re-run ts-fx complexity + layout/graph scans to confirm the dead-code hotspots and the utils->core edge are gone.
